title: Review the uncommitted changes. Context: # Coder Handoff ## Task Review failed: review score 4/10 (min 7) issues to fix: - Then list up to 5 concrete fix items, one per line, using numbered or '-' bullets. - If score >= 7, do not include an issues list. - Format your final line EXACTLY as: SCORE: N/...
summary_text: 1. [assets/hooks/context-monitor.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/hooks/context-monitor.sh):17 and [assets/hooks/context-monitor.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/hooks/context-monitor.sh):53 weaken the context-safety path. The threshold was doubled from `30` to `60`, and the emitted message no longer tells the operator to run `/handover`; it now only suggests considering `/compact` later, while the log still records `handover_suggested`. That is a behavior regression in a safety hook, not just a wording change. 2. [assets/skills/research-loop/eval-skill.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/research-loop/eval-skill.sh):381 makes the phantom-command detector materially less effective by allowlisting nonstandard binaries such as `claude`, `codex`, `cmux`, `pm2`, `launchctl`, and `systemctl`. Those names are no longer verified with `command -v`, so hallucinated commands using those binaries will now pass the anti-hallucination check. 3. [assets/skills/research-loop/eval-skill.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/research-loop/eval-skill.sh):350 introduces a false-negative path for env-prefixed commands. A line like `FOO=$BAR ./script.sh` is now treated as a pure assignment and skipped entirely, whereas the previous implementation still stripped the env vars and validated `./script.sh`. That reduces coverage of the phantom-command penalty. 4. [assets/skills/fugue/experience.jsonl](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/fugue/experience.jsonl), [assets/skills/handover/experience.jsonl](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/handover/experience.jsonl), and related state files add large amounts of generated telemetry and local runtime churn with no functional change. That makes the patch hard to review and harder to revert cleanly. ISSUES TO FIX: 1. Restore the context-monitor’s actual handover trigger semantics, or rename the action/state so the hook behavior and telemetry no longer contradict each other. 2. Remove nonstandard binaries from the `eval-skill.sh` allowlist and keep verifying them with `command -v`. 3. Fix env-prefixed command parsing so forms like `VAR=$OTHER cmd` still validate `cmd` instead of being skipped. 4. Drop generated `experience.jsonl` and local state churn from this patch, or move them out of tracked files. 5. If the new JSON hook output is intentional, add a test or contract check proving `UserPromptSubmit` actually consumes `systemMessage` as intended. SCORE: 4/10
